title: Rotating Standing Photo Display
summary: A small tabletop photo stand on a lazy susan base — spin it to show a different photo without picking it up, using double-sided tape to swap prints.
description:
This is a genuinely small build: a 1x6 base, a 1x8 photo panel standing upright, and a short 1x8 support cut to fit that braces the panel at the back. **The lazy susan hardware is what makes this a rotating display rather than a plain photo stand** — mount it between the base and the upright panel so the whole thing spins freely on a tabletop.

**One support piece is measured and cut to fit rather than built to a fixed length** — the exact angle and length depend on how the upright panel is braced, so dry-fit before committing to a final cut.

Attach photos to the panel face with double-sided tape so they can be swapped out easily, and finish the wood before mounting the hardware, since it's much easier to sand and finish flat pieces than an assembled, spinning stand.

cutList.note: Measure and cut to fit your own assembly.

steps.title: Cut and finish the parts
steps.body: Cut the base, photo panel, and fixed support to size, dry-fitting the second support piece before final cutting. Sand and finish all parts.

steps.title: Assemble the stand
steps.body: Nail the photo panel and its supports together, then attach the assembly to the lazy susan hardware and the base.

steps.title: Add the photos
steps.body: Attach photos to the panel face with double-sided tape, swapping as desired.

unresolvedImages.alt: Rotating standing photo display on a lazy susan base
